findings.title: learn:update-progress only maintains progress convenience arrays for completed/in_progress
findings.impact: Statuses used by learn-plan/learn-execute (skipped/optional/pending) can leave progress.json arrays stale, confusing downstream consumers.
findings.recommendation: Either extend progress schema to model all statuses, or remove the arrays and derive views from knowledge_point_progress.

findings.title: learn:update-state does not validate active_profile_id/active_session_id format or existence
findings.impact: State can be set to invalid IDs, causing subsequent learn commands to fail in harder-to-debug ways.
findings.recommendation: Validate ID format (PROFILE_ID_RE/SESSION_ID_RE) and (optionally) existence when updating state.

findings.title: Strong: atomicWriteJson validates schema before write and keeps backups for recovery
findings.snippet: // Validate *before* writing the final file. const ok = validate(data);
findings.impact: Reduces risk of corrupt learn workflow state and improves crash safety.
findings.recommendation: Extend this pattern to progress.json with a dedicated schema.

areas_covered: CLI state API correctness (lock + atomic write + validation)
areas_remaining: Plan/schema alignment enforcement in state updates

leads.description: Check learn-plan.schema.json status enums vs update-progress behavior
